headline: Berkshire’s OxyChem Deal Reframes Valuation And Earnings Outlook For BRK.B
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) bought Occidental Petroleum's OxyChem unit for US$9.7b, its largest acquisition in years. The deal was Warren Buffett's final major transaction as CEO and closed before a rally in oil prices. OxyChem's value is reported to be at least 30% higher after closing, reflecting the impact of stronger oil markets. Occidental Petroleum used the transaction to reduce debt and raise its dividend, while Berkshire increased its exposure to the energy and chemicals...
